response:
Sec. 1  
peanuts are a type of food that contains proteins which can trigger an allergic reaction in some individuals  
the human body has a system that detects harmful substances and prevents the immune system from reacting to them  
allergies are usually caused by proteins that are found in foods like peanuts  
the immune system of someone with an allergy reacts to these proteins by releasing chemicals that cause symptoms  

Sec. 2  
the chemicals released during an allergic reaction can cause a range of symptoms from mild to severe  
underwater environments do not contain the proteins that trigger allergies  
the human body is not adapted to live in water and would not function properly in such an environment  
the physical properties of water make it difficult for the body to regulate temperature and maintain normal functions
